hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2704  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا مِن ْ ج َّسَّذِةِ ق َّا ِذ َّإ إ َّرَّإذَّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّحَّوِّلَّ ق َّمْلَّه ً مِن ْ مَّكَّان ٍ إ ِلَّى مَّكَّان ٍ ق َّلاَّ ي َّص ُّز َّةُّ.
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Abu al-Jarud, who said: A man asked Abu Ja'far {a.s} about 
a person in the state of ihram who killed a louse.  
Imam {a.s} said: "What a bad thing he has done."  
The man asked: "What is its expiation?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "There is no expiation for it."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 362 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 651 • Wasa’il 
Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 170  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2703
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٧٠٣

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2705   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن َّان   عَّن ْ ر ُّرَّإرَّة َّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ هَّلْ ي َّحُّك  ُّ رَّإ ْسَّهُّ إ َّوْ ي َّع ْسِلُّ ن ِالْمَّاءِ ق َّق َّال 
ي َّي َّعَّم َّذْ ق َّي ْلَّ ذَّإن  َّه ٍ وَّ لأَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِا َّن ْ ي َّع ْي َّسِلَّ ن ِالْمَّاءِ وَّ ي َّصُّث  َّ عَّلَّى رَّإ ْسَّهُّ مَّا لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ مُّلَّن ِّذإً ق َّ ا ِن ْ كَّان َّ مُّلَّن ِّذإً ق َّلاَّ ي ُّق ِي ص ُّ  عَّلَّى  
رَّإ ْسِهِ إَّلْمَّاءَّ إ ِلأ َّ مِن ِ إِجْي ِلاَّمٍ.
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said: "A person 
in the state of ihram should remove all insects from themselves, except for lice, as 
they are from their body. However, if they wish to move a louse from one place to 
another, it does not harm them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 336 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 
653 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 540  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2704

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2706   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ ي َّعْق ُّوب ُّ ن ْن ُّ س ُّعَّي ْث ٍ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي َّع ْي َّسِلُّ ق َّق َّال 
رَّإ ْسِهِ وَّ لأَّ ي َّذْلُّكُّه
hadiths.en:
Aban narrated from Zurara, who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about a person in the 
state of ihram - can they scratch their head or wash it with water?  
Imam {a.s} said: "They may scratch their head as long as they do not intentionally kill an 
insect, and there is no harm in washing with water or pouring it over their head, as long 
as their head is not treated with a binding substance (muddied). If their head is treated 
with such a substance, they should not pour water over it unless it is due to a state of 
ritual impurity (from a wet dream)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2705

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2707   -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ جَّزِي ز ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال إَّلْمُّحْزِمُّ مِن َّ إَّلْح َّن َّان َّه ِ صَّث  َّ عَّلَّى رَّإ ْسِهِ 
إَّلْمَّاءَّ وَّ ي َّمِي ز ُّ إَّلس  َّعْزَّ ن ِا َّن َّامِلِهِ ن َّعْص َّهُّ مِن ْ ن َّعْص ٍ
hadiths.en:
Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a person in the state of 
ihram taking a bath.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, they may pour water over their head, but they should not scrub 
it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2706

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2708  -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي َّس ْهَّذُّ ن ِكَّاحَّ مُّحِل َّي ْن ِ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم  ق َّالَّ  «
إ َّن ْ ي ُّس ِي زَّ ن ِصَّي ْذٍ عَّلَّى مُّحِل    
ق ال مصن ف  هذ إ إلكي اب  -  رص ى  إلله عن ه  -: وهذ إ على إلأن كار لذ لك  لأ على إ ن ه ي ح ور .
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Hariz from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, Imam {a.s} said:  
"When a person in the state of ihram performs ghusl (ritual bath) for janabah (major 
ritual impurity), they should pour water over their head and separate their hair with 
their fingers, parting it gently."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 313 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 
12, p. 536  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[   
THE MUHRIM MARRIES, ARRANGES A MARRIAGE, OR DIVORCES

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2707
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٧٠٧

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2709 - وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال و َّح َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّز َّوِّح َّ مُّحِلاًّ 
ق َّا ِن ْ ي َّز َّو َّح َّ إ َّوْ ر َّو َّح َّ ق َّي َّز ْوِي ح ُّهُّ ن َّاطِل
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said regarding a person in the state of ihram attending the marriage 
of those not in ihram: "They should not witness it."  
Then Imam {a.s} said: "It is permissible for a person in the state of ihram to point out 
game to someone not in ihram."  
[AL SADUQ] 
The compiler of this book - may Allah {SWT} be pleased with him - commented: This is 
stated as a condemnation of such actions, not as an allowance.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 315 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 
674 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 417  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2708

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said: "A person in the 
state of ihram is not allowed to marry or to officiate the marriage of someone who is 
not in ihram. If they marry or officiate a marriage, the marriage is invalid."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 328 • Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 
193 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 676 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 436  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2709
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
A man from the Ansar married while he was in the state of ihram, and the 
Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) invalidated 
his marriage.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 372 • Da’aim al-Islam, Part 2, p. 237 • 
Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 328 • Al-Istibsar, Part 2, p. 193 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 673 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 
12, p. 437 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 14, p. 411  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2710
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٧١٠

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Whoever marries a woman while in the state of ihram, they 
shall be separated, and she will never be permissible for him."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 674 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 440  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2713  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ عَّاصِمِ ن ْن ِ جُّمَّي ْذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّال سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول 
وَّ لأَّ ي َّي َّز َّو َّح
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Sama'ah: "She is entitled to the dowry if he had 
consummated the marriage with her."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2712

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2714   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّعِي ذ إَّلْأ َّعْزَّح ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّن ْز ِلُّ إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة َّ مِن َّ إَّلْمَّحْمِلِ ق َّي َّص ُّم ُّهَّ ا إ ِلَّي ْهِ وَّ هُّوَّ 
مُّحْزِم  ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Asim ibn Humayd from Abu Basir, he said: I heard Imam 
Abu Abdullah {a.s} say: "A person in the state of ihram may pronounce divorce but may 
not marry."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 372 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 383 
• Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 677 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 441  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2713

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2715   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى ِّ ق َّالَّ : ق ُّلْث ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلْمُّحْزِمُّ ي َّن ْط ُّزُّ إ ِل ى  إِمْزَّإ َّي ِهِ وَّ هِى َّ مُّحْزِمَّه   
ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Saeed al-A'raj asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about a man who helps a woman down 
from her mount and holds her close while in the state of ihram.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it unless he does so intentionally [with 
inappropriate intent], and he has more right to help her down than others."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2714

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2716  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ ج َّالِذٍ ن َّي  َّاعِ إَّلْق َّلاَّن ِسِ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْث ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّي َّى إ َّهْلَّهُّ وَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ طَّوَّإف ُّ  
إَّلن ِّسَّاءِ ق َّال ق َّسَّا َّلَّهُّ عَّن ْهَّا ق َّق َّال 
ق َّق ُّلْث ُّ ن َّعْذَّ مَّا   ق َّامُّوإ إ َّصْلَّحَّك َّ إَّلل َّهُّ كَّي ْف َّ ق ُّلْث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ ن َّذَّن َّه   ق َّق َّال ن َّق َّزَّة   وَّ عَّلَّى  
إَّلْق َّق ِي زِ س َّاة  .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Muhammad al -Halabi, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} 
about a man in the state of ihram looking at his wife while she is also in the state of 
ihram.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 699 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 435  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2715

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar: 2717 - وق ال علي ه إلسلام: " لأ ي ذ ن ح إلصي ذ ق ى  إلحزم وإ ن  صي ذ ق ى  إلحل
hadiths.en:
It is narrated from Khalid, the seller of caps, who said: I asked Imam Abu 
Abdullah {a.s} about a man who had intercourse with his wife before completing the 
Tawaf al-Nisa.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He must offer a camel (as expiation)."  
Then another person came and asked Imam {a.s} about the same situation. 
Imam {a.s} said: "He must offer a cow."  
Then another person came and asked him.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He must offer a sheep."  
I said to Imam {a.s} after they had left: "May Allah {SWT} set you right, how is it that you said 
a camel for one, a cow for another, and a sheep for the last?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "For the wealthy, it is a camel; for one of moderate means, it is a cow; 
and for the poor, it is a sheep."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 688 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 13, p. 123  
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
[   
WHAT THE MUHRIM IS PERMITTED TO KILL

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2716

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2718  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّن َّان ُّ ن ْن ُّ سَّذِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال هِ ن ِق َّي ْلِ 
إَّلْق َّا ْرَّة ِ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّزَّمِ وَّ إَّلْأ َّق ْعَّى وَّ إَّلْعَّق ْزَّب ِ وَّ إَّلْع ُّزَّإب ِ إَّلْأ َّن ْق َّغِ ي َّزْمِي هِ ق َّا ِن ْ إ َّصَّن ْي َّهُّ ق َّا َّ ن ْعَّذَّةُّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ وَّ كَّان َّ ي ُّسَّمِّى  إَّلْق َّا ْرَّة َّ 
إَّلْق ُّوَّي ْسِق َّه ِ وَّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Game should not be slaughtered in the Sacred Sanctuary 
(Haram), even if it was hunted outside the sanctuary."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2717
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2719  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال إذَّ عَّن ْ ن َّعِي زِةِ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ 
وَّ لأَّ ي ُّلْق ِى  إَّلْحَّلَّمَّه َّ.
hadiths.en:
Hanan ibn Sadir narrated from Abu Ja'far {a.s} that he said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) 
commanded the killing of a mouse in the Sacred Sanctuary, as well as a snake, a 
scorpion, and a pied crow. If you strike it, may Allah {SWT} distance it [from His mercy]."  
He {saws} used to call the mouse "al -Fuwaysiqah" (the little corrupt one) and said: "It 
weakens water-skins and sets houses on fire for their inhabitants."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 126 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 547  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2718
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٧١٨

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar: 2720 - وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ جَّزِي ز ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  إَّلْحَّلَّمَّه َّ مِن َّ إَّلْن َّعِي زِ
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"If a person in the state of ihram removes ticks from their camel, there is no harm in 
that, but they should not remove lice."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 338 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 
654 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 542  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2719
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٨، ح ٢٧١٩

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2721  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي َّن ْز ِعُّ إَّلْحَّلَّمَّه َّ ع ن ِ إَّلْن َّعِي زِ ق َّق َّال 
هِى َّ ن ِمَّن ْز ِلَّه ِ إَّلْق َّمْلَّه ِ مِن ْ ج َّسَّذِك
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Hariz from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, he said:  
"Ticks are not considered part of the camel, but lice are considered part of the camel."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 654 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 543  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2720
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2722  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلِ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ وَّ مَّا ي ق ْي ُّلُّ مِن َّ  إَّلذ َّوَّإب ِّ 
ق َّال ق ْي ُّلْهُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي ُّزِذْك َّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّق ْي ُّلْهُّ وَّ 
إَّلْكَّلْث ُّ إَّلْعَّق ُّورُّ إ ِن ْ إ َّرَّإذَّك َّ ق َّاق ْي ُّلْهُّ وَّ لأَّ ن َّا ْسَّ لِلْ مُّحْزِمِ إ َّن ْ ي َّزْمِى َّ إَّلْحِذَّإ َّة َّ وَّ إ ِن ْ عَّزَّض َّ لَّهُّ إَّلل ُّصُّوضُّ إِمْي َّن َّغَّ مِن ْهُّمْ
hadiths.en:
In the narration of Ali ibn Abi Hamzah from Abu Basir, he said: I asked  Imam 
{a.s} about a person in the state of ihram removing lice from a camel.  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, it is like the lice from your own body."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 654 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 543  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2721

hadiths.chapter: 118. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl narrated from Abu al-Hasan {a.s}, who said: I asked 
him about what a person in the state of ihram is permitted to kill from among the 
creatures.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They may kill black snakes, vipers, mice, scorpions, and any snake. If 
a predatory animal attacks you, kill it; but if it does not attack, do not kill it. As for a 
rabid dog, if it attacks you, kill it. There is no harm for a person in ihram to throw stones 
at a kite. If robbers confront them, they may defend themselves."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 13, p. 705 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 547

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 118, H. 2722
